tasks.description: Refactor a class with multiple code smells including Long Method, Feature Envy, and duplicated logic to improve readability and reduce complexity

scoring.5: Identifies all major smells: Long Method, Feature Envy (customer access), duplicated discount logic, Switch Statements (loyalty tier), and computes baseline cyclomatic complexity
scoring.3: Identifies 2-3 smells but misses some or does not compute metrics
scoring.1: Identifies only 1 smell or describes issues vaguely without naming specific smells

scoring.5: Presents ordered, incremental steps: extract discount calculation, extract tax calculation, extract validation, move discount logic to customer/strategy, eliminate duplication; each step has clear rationale
scoring.3: Provides a refactoring plan but steps are not well-ordered or some are missing
scoring.1: Vague suggestions like 'split the method' without specific steps
scoring.0: No plan provided; jumps directly to rewriting

scoring.5: Extracted methods/classes are well-named and cohesive; discount duplication eliminated via shared method or Strategy pattern; Feature Envy resolved by moving logic to appropriate class; complexity significantly reduced
scoring.3: Some extractions performed but naming is poor, or duplication is only partially resolved
scoring.1: Minimal structural changes; code is rearranged but smells remain

scoring.5: Explicitly verifies that all discount calculations, tax rates, validation errors, inventory reservations, and email sending produce identical results; notes which tests would cover each behavior
scoring.3: Claims equivalence but does not verify specific behaviors or misses edge cases
scoring.1: No equivalence verification; changes may alter behavior
scoring.0: Introduces obvious behavioral changes without acknowledging them

scoring.5: Reports before/after cyclomatic complexity, cognitive complexity, and duplication metrics; achieves 40%+ complexity reduction; duplication eliminated
scoring.3: Reports some metrics but improvement is modest (20-39%) or not all metrics are measured
scoring.1: No metrics reported; improvement is claimed but not quantified
